title: The Man Who Laughs

evidence.note: Published 1928; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Man Who Laughs” (renewals ~1955, 1956, 1957)
Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Man Who Laughs is a 1928 American silent film directed by Paul Leni for Universal Pictures, adapted from Victor Hugo's novel, starring Conrad Veidt as a man whose face is fixed in a permanent grin. As a 1928 publication, the film print is in the US public domain by expiry of the 95-year term; the underlying Hugo material and any separate character rights are assessed on their own layers.
